questions.question: Does the school district have school gardens, and how are they used
questions.answer: The statute does not specifically mention whether the school district has school gardens or how they are used. It primarily discusses a policy giving preference to native plants on campus.

questions.question: Does the school district follow any practices for using native perennials or controlling invasive weeds in landscaping?
questions.answer: Yes, the school district has a policy to give preference to native plants, which likely includes native perennials, and to replace existing non-native and invasive plants to an appropriate and practicable extent. Decisions on using native plants are based on site conditions.
